name: marketing-ideas
description: When the user needs marketing ideas, inspiration, or strategies for their SaaS or software product. Also use when the user asks for 'marketing ideas,' 'growth ideas,' 'how to market,' 'marketing strategies,' 'marketing tactics,' 'ways to promote,' or 'ideas to grow.' This skill provides 140 proven marketing approaches organized by category.
capabilities: Provide 140 proven marketing ideas organized by 15 categories
Match marketing tactics to company stage and budget
Suggest tactics based on available resources and timeline
Explain implementation steps for recommended ideas
Suggest complementary marketing skills for specific tactics
author: coreyhaines31
securitySummary: All static findings are false positives. Scanner triggered on benign marketing terminology: 'algorithm' in 'programmatic SEO' and 'audit' in 'SEO audit' contexts. No actual cryptographic code or system reconnaissance code exists. This is a text-based marketing strategy skill.
valueStatement: SaaS companies struggle to discover effective marketing tactics that fit their budget and stage. This skill provides 140 proven marketing ideas organized by category, budget, timeline, and growth stage to help founders and marketers find the right approach.
limitations: Does not execute marketing campaigns or access external platforms
Does not provide real-time data or competitive intelligence
Does not create actual content, ads, or marketing assets
Recommendations are general guidance, not customized strategies

useCases.title: Early-Stage Founders Seeking First Marketing Steps
useCases.description: Founders with limited budget and team can discover free and low-cost marketing tactics like content marketing, SEO, and community building that fit their constraints.

useCases.title: Marketing Teams Evaluating New Channels
useCases.description: Marketing managers exploring new acquisition channels can review tactics organized by paid advertising, partnerships, or community-led growth to find expansion opportunities.

useCases.title: Product Teams Adding Marketing Features
useCases.description: Product teams building free tools or calculators as marketing channels can get implementation guidance for engineering-as-marketing tactics.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Marketing Idea Request
promptTemplates.prompt: I need marketing ideas for my SaaS product. My target audience is [describe audience], and my main goal is [describe goal, e.g., acquire first 100 users]. What should I focus on?
promptTemplates.scenario: When user asks for marketing ideas without providing context

promptTemplates.title: Budget-Constrained Tactics
promptTemplates.prompt: I have a limited marketing budget of $[amount] per month. What marketing tactics would you recommend for my [describe product]? I can dedicate about [hours] hours per week.

promptTemplates.title: Stage-Appropriate Strategy
promptTemplates.prompt: Our company is in the [pre-launch/early stage/growth stage/scale stage]. We have [team size] people and [describe resources]. What marketing ideas fit our stage?
promptTemplates.scenario: When user mentions company stage and resources

promptTemplates.title: Channel-Specific Tactics
promptTemplates.prompt: I want to focus on [email marketing/content marketing/paid ads/community building/ partnerships]. What specific tactics should I try for my [describe product] targeting [describe audience]?
promptTemplates.scenario: When user wants to focus on specific marketing channels

bestPractices: Match tactics to your current stage. Early-stage companies should prioritize organic channels while building proof points before investing in paid acquisition.
Consider your available resources honestly. Some tactics require significant time investment (content, community) while others need budget (ads, events).
Start with one or two channels and execute well. Spreading efforts across many tactics often leads to poor results in all of them.
antiPatterns: Trying every tactic at once instead of focusing on a few channels with proper execution.
Ignoring your actual budget and timeline when choosing tactics that require significant investment.
Skipping customer research and using tactics that do not align with where your target audience spends time.

faq.question: What is the best marketing tactic for a pre-launch SaaS?
faq.answer: Focus on content marketing, SEO, and community building. These free channels build audience and authority before you launch. Build an email list through waitlist referrals and early-access campaigns.

faq.question: How much budget do I need for effective marketing?
faq.answer: Free tactics exist for every stage. With zero budget, focus on content, SEO, and community. Paid ads can work with $1,000-5,000 monthly. Scale budgets depend on your LTV and conversion rates.

faq.question: How long until marketing tactics show results?
faq.answer: Quick wins like ads and email can generate results in days to weeks. Medium-term tactics like content and community take 3-6 months. Long-term strategies like brand building and thought leadership take 12+ months.

faq.question: Can this skill create marketing content for me?
faq.answer: No. This skill provides tactical guidance and strategy recommendations. It does not write actual ad copy, blog posts, or marketing assets. Use writing skills for content creation.

faq.question: How do I choose between marketing channels?
faq.answer: Consider where your target audience spends time, your budget, team skills, and timeline. B2B SaaS often starts with LinkedIn and content. B2C works well with social media and paid ads on relevant platforms.

faq.question: What skills work well with marketing-ideas?
faq.answer: Use programmatic-seo for scaling SEO content, email-sequence for email marketing tactics, competitor-alternatives for comparison pages, and free-tool-strategy for engineering-as-marketing approaches.
